name: R55 v3 full-corpus standalone validation
hypothesis: the completed 656-row census preserves canonical coverage, frozen prefix, mixed timing gates, artifact hashes, and dual-enumerator summaries
expected_signal: standalone validator exits zero and writes status=valid bound to the final manifest hash
decision_value: independently validates the complete supplied-corpus census before the residual-SAT discriminator

efficiency_design.naive_cost: Re-enumerating every embedding with both producers would repeat the completed multi-hour census. The validation target is instead the immutable 656-row manifest, its 656 compressed host artifacts, and the preserved dual-enumerator summaries.
opportunities_considered: Stream the JSONL manifest once rather than loading or regenerating the corpus search.
Hash and decompress each compact per-host artifact exactly once.
Reuse the already recorded bitset/NetworkX stream agreement while independently checking the stored streams, counts, summaries, paths, and hashes.
Verify the frozen 254-row prefix by its raw-line aggregate hash and every row by its individual artifact hash.
Write one atomic validation result bound to the final manifest hash.
Reserve fresh semantic re-enumeration for the separately implemented cold residual audit.
efficiency_design.chosen_reductions: Perform a standalone single-pass integrity and semantic-consistency validation of all 656 canonical rows and gzip artifacts. Enforce the 30-second gate for hosts 0..253, the 60-second gate for hosts 254..655, the frozen original-prefix hash, sequential coverage, exact artifact paths and hashes, and stored bitset/NetworkX agreement.
efficiency_design.expected_throughput_gain: Avoids repeating roughly 4-6 core-hours of embedding enumeration; expected validation is bounded to one <=600-second, <=1000-MB job while still rehashing every final artifact.
efficiency_design.soundness_basis: The validator is a separate executable and parser. It binds the final manifest SHA-256, rechecks every declared artifact SHA-256 after gzip decoding, enforces canonical source coverage and mixed timing gates, and rejects malformed or inconsistent summaries. Its exact command and execution receipt will be independently hash-bound before operator validation.
efficiency_design.remains_uncompressed: This validator does not freshly enumerate embeddings. Its conclusion is full-corpus artifact integrity and stored dual-enumerator agreement, not an independent re-performance of NetworkX. The later cold residual audit supplies that stronger semantic independence.
